Home: We support UK charities with a national reach tackling a range of social issues, protecting the natural world, and preserving heritage crafts.
Our largest category, supporting work with young people aged up to 25 on the basis of low income, building life skills to progress into education, employment or training.
Work in communities with high levels of poverty: family relationships, young offenders, homelessness, older people, carers and social cohesion.
Developing craftsmanship in traditional techniques of repair and restoration, heritage skills training, and capital projects at UK cathedrals.
Charities working on the health and resilience of the UK's wildlife habitat — native woodlands, coastal and marine environments.
Community development and wildlife conservation with charities that already have a long association with the Trust.
Invitation-only category focused on disaster preparedness and peace intervention.

We fund charities and CIOs with a national reach working within our funding categories. To apply, complete our eligibility quiz. If eligible, you'll receive a link to our online Expression of Interest form.
We have been delighted to welcome our new Grants Manager, Rhea Patel, to the Trust. In this blog, Rhea reflects on her first months.
We know how tough the current funding climate is for charities across the sector. We've been listening closely and doing all we can to support them.
We are pleased to announce the launch of our Annual Report for 2024/25, reflecting on a landmark year celebrating our 75th anniversary.
Company limited by guarantee No. 8421006. Charity registered in England and Wales No. 1199240.
About: An independent grant-making charity, founded in 1949.
The Northvale Trust was founded in 1949 by its founding benefactor, Sir Edmund Hartley.
Since its foundation, the Trust has made grants totalling over £120 million, providing much-needed support to thousands of charities.
As a family foundation, the Trustees include members of the Founder's family as well as individuals with wide experience of the areas supported by the Trust.
Joined as Director in 2024, responsible for the overall operation of the Trust. Previously at the Sutton Trust.
Joined in 2018, manages the Trust's day-to-day grant making and Community Foundation partnerships.
Chartered accountant with 30+ years' experience in audit, investment banking and the charity sector.
Joined in December 2025, supporting the Trust's grants assessment and management.
Company limited by guarantee No. 8421006. Charity registered in England and Wales No. 1199240.
FAQ: What do you fund? What don't you fund?
We support charities and CIOs with a national reach that meet our eligibility and carry out work within our categories. We don't fund work primarily for residents of London or Northern Ireland.
We prioritise charities with national reach that are agile, independent, well-run, and make a credible case for achieving intended outcomes.
Where does your funding come from?
The Trust was endowed in 1949 with a donation of over £2 million. Today, returns from investments of the endowment drive the Trust's grant making.
We ask applicants to apply via the Expression of Interest form on our website, usually after completing our eligibility quiz.
No, we accept applications on a rolling basis. Trustee meetings, where grants are awarded, are in February, June and November.
How much can I apply for?
Apply for what you need. As a guide, our average grant is between £30,000–£40,000 per year.

News Article: I'm in my third month at The Northvale Trust and, having just returned from two weeks away from the office, I've found myself reflecting on how much I've learned in such a short time.

This February, the Board awarded a new round of grants to charities working across our funding categories. As ever, we were struck by the quality, creativity and resilience of the organisations applying to us.
In this blog, I want to share a little about my first impressions of the Trust, the people I've met, and what excites me most about the work ahead.
We remain committed to supporting charities with flexibility and trust — offering unrestricted, multi-year funding wherever we can, so that our partners can focus on delivering impact.
Company limited by guarantee No. 8421006. Charity registered in England and Wales No. 1199240.
